template.title: Slack Fronting Starter
template.summary: Template-suggested Slack API/MCP fronting structure with reviewed capability candidates.
template.description: Starts a governed Slack fronting project from reviewed starter data. Verify backend operations, auth posture, and policy boundaries against the real integration before locking.
template.recommendedBrief: Create a governed ANIP fronting layer for Slack. Expose selected behavior-shaped capabilities instead of raw backend operations. Read operations must preserve actor-visible scope. Write-adjacent operations must prepare previews or require approval before downstream mutation.

documents.title: Slack governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Slack Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `slack-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Slack capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- slack.channel.read_context: Read bounded recent Slack channel messages for declared context without exposing raw workspace tools.
- slack.thread.summarize: Retrieve bounded Slack thread replies and return context for summarization without posting.
- slack.message.prepare: Prepare a Slack channel message preview without sending it.
- slack.incident_update.prepare: Prepare a structured incident update for a Slack channel and stop before sending.
- slack.announcement.request: Prepare a channel announcement request that requires approval before posting.

## Governance posture
- Raw Slack tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

documents.title: Slack integration evidence
documents.content:
# Slack Integration Evidence Template

> Imported from starter template `slack-fronting-starter`. Verify every operation against your chosen native API/MCP source before locking.

Native API connection: SLACK_API_URL
MCP connection: SLACK_MCP_ENDPOINT

## Template-suggested backend operations
- slack.web.read_channel_context: GET /slack/channels/{channel_id}/context
- slack.web.summarize_thread: GET /slack/channels/{channel_id}/threads/{thread_ts}/summary
- slack.web.prepare_channel_message: POST /slack/channels/{channel_id}/messages/preview
- slack.web.prepare_incident_update: POST /slack/channels/{channel_id}/incident-updates/preview
- slack.web.request_channel_announcement: POST /slack/channels/{channel_id}/announcements/request

## Secret posture
- Studio stores secret references only; project exports must not contain tokens.
- Teams can implement either native API or MCP backend seams behind the same governed ANIP contract.

documents.title: Slack developer evidence
documents.content:
# Slack Developer Evidence

Imported from starter template `slack-fronting-starter`. This is developer-owned evidence for Studio Autopilot and generated-service review.

This document turns starter operations into explicit ANIP runtime governance and input-contract evidence. Review it against the real integration before publishing.

## Capability Evidence Summary

| capability_id | service_id | operation_type | side_effect_level | inputs | backend_operation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | --- |
| slack.channel.read_context | slack-fronting | read | read | 4 | slack.web.read_channel_context |
| slack.thread.summarize | slack-fronting | read | read | 4 | slack.web.summarize_thread |
| slack.message.prepare | slack-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 4 | slack.web.prepare_channel_message |
| slack.incident_update.prepare | slack-fronting | write | write_adjacent | 6 | slack.web.prepare_incident_update |
| slack.announcement.request | slack-governance | write | write_adjacent | 4 | slack.web.request_channel_announcement |

## Reviewed Developer Evidence

documents.title: Slack governed fronting intent
documents.content:
# Slack Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `slack-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Slack capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- slack.channel.read_context: Read bounded recent Slack channel messages for declared context without exposing raw workspace tools.
- slack.thread.summarize: Retrieve bounded Slack thread replies and return context for summarization without posting.
- slack.message.prepare: Prepare a Slack channel message preview without sending it.
- slack.incident_update.prepare: Prepare a structured incident update for a Slack channel and stop before sending.
- slack.announcement.request: Prepare a channel announcement request that requires approval before posting.

## Governance posture
- Raw Slack tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.
